threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Seeing you weigh your shopping bags optimally to catch the 243 bus home. Trust me, it's all about that bus stop showdown at peak hour.
Saw a guy serenading a pigeon on the 45 last night. It gave him a head tilt like it was actually impressed. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a couple arguing passionately in Turkish while their dog casually dug up a neighbor's garden. Pure Neukölln chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you grab a döner at 3 AM and then stumble into an open-air rave in a former factory. It’s a wild combo.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their unapologetic sidestep around the inevitable pigeon poop while holding a BART coffee. Tourists just step right in it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing like a dog holding up a Muni train at Civic Center. Everyone grumbling, but that pooch is living the dream, soaking up all that attention.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Many tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí's playground. They miss our local festivals like La Festa Major, where real magic happens without the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still fumble with the 'tap and pay' on TMB. Locals have that swipe-down motion perfected.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you're waiting for a train at Hackney Central. Proper locals know the difference.
Last night on the 196, a guy argued passionately with the driver about his lost Oyster card – never seen someone plead like that over a bus fare.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy juggling on the sidewalk while a group of kids traded Pokémon cards nearby, all while the BVG strike news blared from a café.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's not just the street art that speaks; the walls have their own conversations during protests. You can almost hear the history echoing.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always tell a local by how they know to avoid Civic Center during a dog-on-the-tracks incident. Tourists just stand there confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 38 during the BART mess—two guys argued about whether the dog on the tracks was more disruptive than the delays. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and plazas, but it's the tiny shops and hidden grafitti that tell the real story of our neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still wearing their hiking boots at the local bar, trying to figure out why the tapas menu doesn’t include anything they recognize.
